skill.name: shellcheck-configuration
skill.description: Configure ShellCheck static analysis for shell scripts, CI pipelines, pre-commit hooks, and project-specific rule policies.
skill.summary: Guides ShellCheck setup, configuration, warning handling, and workflow integration for shell script quality.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, network, and blocker patterns, but review shows they are documentation examples for ShellCheck usage. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, persistence, or malicious execution intent was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Examples Are Documentation
low_findings.description: The external command detections occur in fenced examples showing how to install ShellCheck, run it locally, add CI checks, and parallelize linting. These commands are relevant to the skill purpose and do not show hidden execution, user-input command injection, or unauthorized behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched lines are visibly fenced examples and workflow snippets for ShellCheck. The semantic context supports legitimate documentation rather than executable malware.

low_findings.title: Configuration File References Are Expected
low_findings.description: The hidden file matches reference .shellcheckrc and shell profile paths as configuration examples. They do not instruct the model to read secrets, modify unrelated user files, or persist unauthorized changes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file paths are standard examples for ShellCheck and shell configuration guidance. No sensitive file harvesting or broad filesystem traversal was found.

low_findings.title: Network URLs Point To Public ShellCheck Resources
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs identify the official ShellCheck repository, wiki, and reference site. They are informational links and do not transmit local data or credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are public ShellCheck resources used for installation and documentation. No outbound request code or exfiltration target appears in the file.

low_findings.title: Blocker Keywords Are ShellCheck Terminology
low_findings.description: The blocker keyword matches come from ShellCheck rule names, disable directives, source annotations, process examples, and command comments. They are not command-and-control instructions or prompt-injection attempts.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms are embedded in normal ShellCheck examples and rule explanations. I found no text instructing the evaluator or assistant to override security rules.

dangerous_patterns.title: Privilege Escalation Command In CI Example
dangerous_patterns.description: A GitHub Actions example uses sudo apt-get install shellcheck. This is a common package installation pattern, but users should review privileged commands before copying workflow examples.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The sudo command is explicit and privileged, but it installs a known linting tool in a CI example. This is a cautionary pattern, not confirmed malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Source Checkout Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The installation section shows cloning the ShellCheck source repository before building it. This is expected setup guidance, but users should verify source repositories before building software.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command downloads code from the public ShellCheck repository. It is legitimate in context, with ordinary supply-chain caution still applicable.

dangerous_patterns.title: Parallel Command Execution Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The performance section uses xargs to run ShellCheck over files in parallel. The command target is fixed to shellcheck, so arbitrary command execution is not indicated.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: xargs can execute commands, but this example uses it for a specific linting command. The context does not show attacker-controlled command construction.

content.value_statement: Shell scripts often fail because quoting, portability, and error handling issues are missed during review. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code configure ShellCheck workflows that catch issues early.
actual_capabilities: Explains ShellCheck installation options for common development environments.
Shows how to create project-level .shellcheckrc settings.
Maps common ShellCheck warning codes to practical fixes.
Provides CI, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and pre-commit integration patterns.
Guides suppression of false positives with documented rule exclusions.
Covers output formats for humans, CI logs, and parsers.
limitations: Does not execute ShellCheck or inspect repository files by itself.
Does not guarantee every shell script is portable across all shells.
Examples may need adjustment for project policies and package managers.
Suppression guidance depends on the exact script context.

use_cases.title: Add Shell Linting To CI
use_cases.description: Create a repeatable ShellCheck job that blocks unsafe shell changes before merge.

use_cases.title: Clean Up Existing Scripts
use_cases.description: Interpret ShellCheck warnings and turn them into clear fixes for Bash or POSIX shell scripts.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Rules
use_cases.description: Define project rule exclusions, target shells, and documentation expectations for script reviews.

prompt_templates.title: Start A ShellCheck Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a repository without current shell linting.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me add ShellCheck to this repository. Recommend a minimal configuration, explain each setting, and list the files I should lint first.

prompt_templates.title: Fix ShellCheck Warnings
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer has warning codes from a script review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain these ShellCheck warnings and suggest safe fixes. Separate portability fixes from Bash-only improvements.

prompt_templates.title: Design CI Enforcement
prompt_templates.scenario: Team wants linting in pull requests.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a ShellCheck CI plan for GitHub Actions or GitLab CI. Include failure behavior, output format, and rule exclusion policy.

prompt_templates.title: Audit A Rule Policy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an existing lint policy.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this .shellcheckrc policy for risk, maintainability, and portability. Identify overbroad exclusions and propose safer alternatives.

output_examples.input: A repository has several Bash scripts and no linting policy.
output_examples.output: A setup plan with target shell choice, recommended optional checks, CI enforcement steps, and a short rollout sequence.

output_examples.input: A script reports SC2086, SC2181, and SC2015 warnings.
output_examples.output: A warning-by-warning explanation with likely root causes, safer rewrite options, and notes about behavior changes to test.

output_examples.input: A team wants to suppress noisy ShellCheck warnings.
output_examples.output: A suppression policy that requires narrow rule scope, inline justification, and periodic review of disabled checks.

best_practices: Choose the target shell before interpreting ShellCheck results.
Document every disabled rule with a clear project reason.
Run ShellCheck in local hooks and CI to catch issues early.
anti_patterns: Disabling broad warning groups without reviewing each warning.
Treating Bash-specific fixes as portable POSIX shell guidance.
Copying privileged installation commands without checking the environment.

faq.question: Does this skill run ShellCheck automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. The user or agent must run ShellCheck in the target environment.

faq.question: Can it help with Bash and POSIX shell scripts?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains how to set the target shell and understand portability warnings.

faq.question: Does it cover CI integration?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns for GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and pre-commit workflows.

faq.question: Can I disable ShellCheck warnings?
faq.answer: Yes, but disabled rules should be narrow, documented, and reviewed periodically.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: The skill itself is documentation. Review any commands before running them in private or sensitive environments.

faq.question: Does it replace manual script testing?
faq.answer: No. ShellCheck catches static issues, but scripts still need functional tests in their real runtime environment.
